exam_price_notes: Approx. 2,200–2,800 RUB (~$25–$31 USD) in Russia; varies internationally

testing_centers.provider: TORFL Certified Test Centres worldwide (~300 centres at universities, Russian cultural institutes, embassies)
Pushkin State Russian Language Institute (Moscow — flagship centre)

id_requirements: Valid government-issued photo ID: passport or national identity card required at the test centre.
exam_rules: Each level consists of five separately timed sub-tests administered in one session. No dictionaries, phones, or reference materials permitted. The speaking sub-test is conducted face-to-face with a trained examiner and is recorded. Candidates who fail up to two sub-tests receive a partial TORFL certificate indicating which sub-tests were passed.
renewal_options: TORFL certificates have lifetime validity — they do not expire.

prerequisites.description: None; recommended ~180–200 hours of Russian study

retake_policy.notes: No official waiting period between attempts. Exam frequency depends on the individual test centre schedule (typically 2–4 sessions per year). Candidates may retake the full exam or only failed sub-tests at participating centres.

domains.name: Лексика и грамматика — Vocabulary & Grammar (~1,300 word vocabulary, case system, verb aspects)
Чтение — Reading (short newspaper items, personal correspondence, advertisements)
Аудирование — Listening (short radio announcements, everyday conversations)
Письмо — Writing (personal letter, postcard, short message)
Говорение — Speaking (describe daily routine, give directions, make simple requests)
